# ASIT — Control Coverage

Version 0.2.0 · 2026-07-24

Maps the 14-control governance map onto the taxonomy. Two kinds of gap are surfaced, not hidden: **controls that mitigate no category** (possibly redundant, or mis-scoped) and **categories that lean on a single control** (thin, brittle coverage).

## Controls and what they cover

| Control | Stage | Name | Categories mitigated |
|---------|-------|------|----------------------|
| CTL-01 | FOUND | Agent Identity | ASIT-FOUND-01 |
| CTL-02 | FOUND | Agent Register | ASIT-FOUND-01 |
| CTL-03 | FOUND | Audit Trail | ASIT-TEST-01, ASIT-OPS-03, ASIT-FOUND-01 |
| CTL-04 | FOUND | Model Supply Chain Integrity | ASIT-BUILD-02, ASIT-BUILD-04, ASIT-REL-02, ASIT-OPS-04, ASIT-FOUND-02 |
| CTL-05 | FOUND | Telemetry | ASIT-OPS-02, ASIT-OPS-03, ASIT-OPS-04 |
| CTL-06 | PLAN | Task Authorization Boundary | ASIT-PLAN-01, ASIT-OPS-01 |
| CTL-07 | DESIGN | Design Risk Gate | ASIT-DESIGN-01 |
| CTL-08 | DESIGN | Least-Privilege Permission Design | ASIT-DESIGN-02, ASIT-BUILD-03, ASIT-OPS-01, ASIT-OPS-04 |
| CTL-09 | BUILD | Context and Prompt Integrity | ASIT-BUILD-03, ASIT-BUILD-04 |
| CTL-10 | BUILD | Human-in-the-Loop Change Approval | ASIT-BUILD-01, ASIT-BUILD-05, ASIT-TEST-01 |
| CTL-11 | TEST | Automated Security and Correctness Testing | ASIT-BUILD-01, ASIT-BUILD-02, ASIT-TEST-01 |
| CTL-12 | REL | Release Gate and Change-Freeze Enforcement | ASIT-REL-01, ASIT-REL-02 |
| CTL-13 | OPS | Runtime Oversight and Kill Switch | ASIT-DESIGN-01, ASIT-OPS-01, ASIT-OPS-02, ASIT-OPS-03 |
| CTL-14 | REL | Release Artifact Attestation | ASIT-BUILD-02, ASIT-REL-02, ASIT-FOUND-02 |

## Gaps

No control is entirely unused.

Every category maps to at least one control.

**Categories mitigated by a single control** — brittle; one control failing leaves the mode uncovered:
- ASIT-PLAN-01 (Under-scoped Task Authorization) → CTL-06
- ASIT-DESIGN-02 (Overbroad Permission or Tool Grant) → CTL-08
- ASIT-BUILD-05 (Unrequested Scope Expansion) → CTL-10
- ASIT-REL-01 (Change-Freeze or Approval-Gate Violation) → CTL-12
